text:
Greek Elections


The United Democratic Left (EDA), legal front for the pro-
scribed Communist party of Greece, is trying to make the
municipal elections scheduled for 5 April a test of the pro-West-
ern government's popularity.

EDA leaders are seeking collaboration of non-Communist
opposition parties in sponsoring joint slates of candidates for
the municipal councils. While EDA has been rebuffed by spokes-
men for several minor nationalist opposition parties, it appar-
ocr_text: The United Democratic Left (EDA), legal front for the pro-scribed Communist party of Greece, is trying to make the municipal elections scheduled for 5 April a test of the pro-West-ern government's popularity.

text:
ently has achieved some success with Liberal leader Sophocles
Venízelos.

This co-
operation, even if not effective in the council elections, may
become evident in the subsequent election of mayors by the
councillors.

Venízelos, however, is spokesman for only one faction of
the rapidly deteriorating Liberal party. Several prominent
Liberals have denounced collaboration with EDA and are work-
ocr_text: This co- operation, even if not effective in the council elections, may become evident in the subsequent election of mayors by the councilors.

text:
ing with other nationalist leaders to defeat the Communists.
This grouping may hold the balance of power in some cities if
the elections result in approximately equal distribution of coun-
cil seats between EDA and the government.

The government favors holding "nonpolitical" elections,
--emphasizing candidates rather than parties--apparently for fear
that EDA, which captured 25 percent of the popular vote in the
general elections in May 1958, has continued to gain strength
ocr_text: The government favors holding "nonpolitical" elections, emphasizing candidates rather than parties - apparently for fear that EDA, which captured 25 percent of the popular vote in the general elections in May 1958, has continued to gain strength among the electorate. EDA will probably gain new respectability from its association with the Venzielos wing of the Liberals. It will take credit for the victories of several non-Communist candidates and will probably increase its representation in local governments throughout Greece.
